checks.name: api

No CORS headers — API may not be accessible from browser-based agents
Server always returns HTML regardless of Accept header — no API content negotiation

checks.name: structure
findings: Heavy div usage: 251 divs with only 29 semantic elements
Poor accessibility: only 89/141 interactive elements have labels
Forms are well-structured: 13/13 inputs have names
No stable test selectors found (data-testid, data-cy, etc.)
Likely a client-side SPA — minimal server-rendered content
Good heading hierarchy: 27 headings, well-structured
5 links with no text or aria-label — invisible to agents
Good link accessibility: 103/108 links are descriptive

checks.name: errors
findings: Non-existent path returned 401 instead of 404
No rate limit headers found
Server accepts DELETE on main page (no method validation)

checks.name: cost
findings: Page is ~48,459 tokens raw — very expensive for agents (>15,000 threshold)
Signal-to-noise ratio is 2% — most of the page is noise
Inline scripts/styles are 0% of page — reasonable
Max DOM depth is 22 — moderately nested
CSS class attributes consume ~10,586 tokens (22% of page) — utility class bloat
Reading this page costs $0.06–$0.73 per load ($0.30–$3.63 for a 5-page session)

checks.name: a11y
findings: Good landmark coverage: banner, contentinfo, main, navigation
All 29 images have alt attributes (14 descriptive, 15 decorative)
No skip links found — agents must traverse entire page to find content
Found 2 live region(s) — agents can detect dynamic content changes
1 custom elements made focusable via tabindex=0
14 elements with tabindex=-1 (programmatic focus)

checks.name: performance
findings: Moderate response time: 512ms
Moderate payload: 189KB
2 redirect(s) — acceptable
Resource heavy: 23 scripts (heavy JS), 14 stylesheets
Compression enabled: gzip
